  • Hide All Code
  • Download Rmd

Modeling

Libraries

library(tidyverse)
library(brms)
library(tidybayes)
library(ggdist)
library(loo)
library(bayesplot)
library(posterior)

dir.create("models", showWarnings = FALSE)

Load Data

pdd <- readRDS("data/processed/pdd_panel.rds")

facility_labels <- c(
  "300225" = "Orange Coast Memorial",
  "301098" = "Anaheim Regional",
  "301175" = "UCI Fountain Valley",
  "301188" = "Anaheim Global",
  "301205" = "Hoag Newport Beach",
  "301209" = "Huntington Beach",
  "301234" = "La Palma",
  "301248" = "UCI Los Alamitos",
  "301258" = "South Coast Global",
  "301262" = "Mission Mission Viejo",
  "301279" = "UCI Orange",
  "301283" = "Garden Grove",
  "301297" = "UCI Placentia Linda",
  "301317" = "Saddleback",
  "301337" = "Mission Laguna Beach",
  "301340" = "St. Joseph",
  "301342" = "St. Jude",
  "301357" = "Foothill Regional",
  "301379" = "West Anaheim",
  "301566" = "OC Global",
  "304409" = "Kaiser Anaheim"
)

pdd <- pdd %>%
  mutate(facility_label = facility_labels[oshpd_id])

cat(nrow(pdd), "rows |", n_distinct(pdd$oshpd_id), "facilities |",
    n_distinct(pdd$data_year), "years\n")
147 rows | 21 facilities | 7 years

Prepare Scorecard Features

Baseline averages (2018-2019) for Elderly Share and Occupancy Rate. Z-scored so coefficients are comparable. Using the early baseline as the fixed reference point — in a real-time monitoring system, these would be updated periodically against the same baseline.

scorecard <- pdd %>%
  filter(data_year %in% c(2018, 2019)) %>%
  group_by(oshpd_id) %>%
  summarise(
    share_60plus_raw = mean(share_60plus, na.rm = TRUE),
    occupancy_raw    = mean(occupancy,    na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    share_60plus_z = as.numeric(scale(share_60plus_raw)),
    occupancy_z    = as.numeric(scale(occupancy_raw))
  )

pdd <- pdd %>%
  left_join(scorecard %>% select(oshpd_id, share_60plus_z, occupancy_z),
            by = "oshpd_id")

cat("Scorecard features - baseline means and SDs:\n")
Scorecard features - baseline means and SDs:
scorecard %>%
  summarise(
    across(ends_with("_raw"),
           list(mean = ~ round(mean(.), 3), sd = ~ round(sd(.), 3)))
  )

Train / Test Split

Training: 2018-2023 (6 years). Test: 2024 (1 year — held out entirely).

train <- pdd %>% filter(data_year <= 2023)
test  <- pdd %>% filter(data_year == 2024)

cat("Train:", nrow(train), "rows |", n_distinct(train$data_year), "years (",
    paste(sort(unique(train$data_year)), collapse = ", "), ")\n")
Train: 126 rows | 6 years ( 2018, 2019, 2020, 2021, 2022, 2023 )
cat("Test: ", nrow(test),  "rows |", n_distinct(test$data_year),  "years (",
    paste(sort(unique(test$data_year)),  collapse = ", "), ")\n")
Test:  21 rows | 1 years ( 2024 )

Priors

# Q1 forecasting model (train only): base — no scorecard features
priors_base <- c(
  prior(normal(log(10000), 0.5), class = Intercept),
  prior(normal(0, 0.05),         class = b, coef = year_idx),
  prior(normal(0, 0.3),          class = sd),
  prior(gamma(4, 0.1),           class = shape)
)

# Q2 forecasting model (train only): scorecard features added
priors_scorecard <- c(
  prior(normal(log(10000), 0.5), class = Intercept),
  prior(normal(0, 0.05),         class = b, coef = year_idx),
  prior(normal(0, 0.5),          class = b, coef = share_60plus_z),
  prior(normal(0, 0.5),          class = b, coef = occupancy_z),
  prior(normal(0, 0.3),          class = sd),
  prior(gamma(4, 0.1),           class = shape)
)

Prior Predictive Check

m_prior <- brm(
  toc_acute ~ year_idx + (1 | oshpd_id),
  data         = train,
  family       = negbinomial(),
  prior        = priors_base,
  sample_prior = "only",
  chains = 2, iter = 1000, warmup = 500,
  seed   = 42,
  file   = "models/m_prior_v2"
)

yrep <- posterior_predict(m_prior, ndraws = 50)
yrep_df <- as.data.frame(t(yrep)) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "draw", values_to = "value")

ggplot() +
  geom_density(data = yrep_df, aes(x = value, group = draw),
               color = "#d7191c", alpha = 0.5, linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_density(data = data.frame(value = train$toc_acute), aes(x = value),
               color = "black", linewidth = 1) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(lineheight = 1.3)) +
  labs(title    = "Prior Predictive Check",
       subtitle = "Red = simulated (prior only); black = observed (train).\nSimulated outcomes should bracket the observed range without blowing up.",
       x = "Total Acute Admissions", y = "Density")

Q1: Facility Vulnerability Forecasting — Base Model

Fit on 2018-2023. Facility random intercepts capture baseline size differences. No scorecard features — this is the baseline predictive model.

m_base <- brm(
  toc_acute ~ year_idx + (1 | oshpd_id),
  data    = train,
  family  = negbinomial(),
  prior   = priors_base,
  chains  = 4, iter = 3000, warmup = 1500,
  control = list(adapt_delta = 0.95),
  seed    = 42,
  file    = "models/m_base_v3"
)

summary(m_base)
 Family: negbinomial 
  Links: mu = log 
Formula: toc_acute ~ year_idx + (1 | oshpd_id) 
   Data: train (Number of observations: 126) 
  Draws: 4 chains, each with iter = 3000; warmup = 1500; thin = 1;
         total post-warmup draws = 6000

Multilevel Hyperparameters:
~oshpd_id (Number of levels: 21) 
              Estimate Est.Error l-95% CI u-95% CI Rhat Bulk_ESS Tail_ESS
sd(Intercept)     0.82      0.10     0.66     1.02 1.01      617      884

Regression Coefficients:
          Estimate Est.Error l-95% CI u-95% CI Rhat Bulk_ESS Tail_ESS
Intercept     9.09      0.17     8.75     9.44 1.02      476      648
year_idx     -0.03      0.01    -0.04    -0.01 1.00     2227     2705

Further Distributional Parameters:
      Estimate Est.Error l-95% CI u-95% CI Rhat Bulk_ESS Tail_ESS
shape    40.49      5.43    30.70    51.89 1.00     1886     2276

Draws were sampled using sampling(NUTS). For each parameter, Bulk_ESS
and Tail_ESS are effective sample size measures, and Rhat is the potential
scale reduction factor on split chains (at convergence, Rhat = 1).

Q2: Facility Vulnerability Forecasting — Scorecard Model

Same structure as m_base but adds baseline occupancy rate and elderly share as fixed predictors. Tests whether structural facility characteristics improve our ability to forecast which facilities are under the most demand pressure.

m_scorecard <- brm(
  toc_acute ~ year_idx + share_60plus_z + occupancy_z + (1 | oshpd_id),
  data    = train,
  family  = negbinomial(),
  prior   = priors_scorecard,
  chains  = 4, iter = 3000, warmup = 1500,
  control = list(adapt_delta = 0.95),
  seed    = 42,
  file    = "models/m_scorecard_v3"
)

summary(m_scorecard)
 Family: negbinomial 
  Links: mu = log 
Formula: toc_acute ~ year_idx + share_60plus_z + occupancy_z + (1 | oshpd_id) 
   Data: train (Number of observations: 126) 
  Draws: 4 chains, each with iter = 3000; warmup = 1500; thin = 1;
         total post-warmup draws = 6000

Multilevel Hyperparameters:
~oshpd_id (Number of levels: 21) 
              Estimate Est.Error l-95% CI u-95% CI Rhat Bulk_ESS Tail_ESS
sd(Intercept)     0.64      0.09     0.49     0.84 1.00     1429     1726

Regression Coefficients:
               Estimate Est.Error l-95% CI u-95% CI Rhat Bulk_ESS Tail_ESS
Intercept          9.08      0.13     8.82     9.33 1.00     1657     2620
year_idx          -0.03      0.01    -0.04    -0.01 1.00     4606     3318
share_60plus_z     0.03      0.16    -0.30     0.35 1.00     2173     2746
occupancy_z        0.63      0.16     0.29     0.95 1.00     1958     2265

Further Distributional Parameters:
      Estimate Est.Error l-95% CI u-95% CI Rhat Bulk_ESS Tail_ESS
shape    40.72      5.55    30.63    52.34 1.00     3343     3069

Draws were sampled using sampling(NUTS). For each parameter, Bulk_ESS
and Tail_ESS are effective sample size measures, and Rhat is the potential
scale reduction factor on split chains (at convergence, Rhat = 1).
# Feature coefficients — practical interpretation
m_scorecard %>%
  spread_draws(b_share_60plus_z, b_occupancy_z) %>%
  pivot_longer(starts_with("b_"), names_to = "feature", values_to = "estimate") %>%
  mutate(
    feature    = recode(feature,
                        "b_share_60plus_z" = "Elderly Share (60+)",
                        "b_occupancy_z"    = "Occupancy Rate"),
    pct_change = (exp(estimate) - 1) * 100
  ) %>%
  group_by(feature) %>%
  median_qi(pct_change, .width = 0.90) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(., 1))) %>%
  transmute(
    Feature                   = feature,
    `Effect per 1-SD (median)` = paste0(pct_change, "%"),
    `90% Lower`               = paste0(.lower, "%"),
    `90% Upper`               = paste0(.upper, "%")
  )
# Posterior distributions of scorecard feature effects (% change in admissions per 1-SD)
m_scorecard %>%
  spread_draws(b_share_60plus_z, b_occupancy_z) %>%
  pivot_longer(starts_with("b_"), names_to = "feature", values_to = "estimate") %>%
  mutate(
    feature    = recode(feature,
                        "b_share_60plus_z" = "Elderly Share (60+)",
                        "b_occupancy_z"    = "Occupancy Rate"),
    pct_change = (exp(estimate) - 1) * 100
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = pct_change, y = feature, fill = feature)) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = c(0.50, 0.90), point_interval = median_qi,
               alpha = 0.85, normalize = "panels") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey30", linewidth = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Elderly Share (60+)" = "#2c7bb6",
                               "Occupancy Rate"       = "#d7191c")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(lineheight = 1.3),
        legend.position = "none") +
  labs(
    title    = "Scorecard Feature Effects on Annual Admissions",
    subtitle = "Posterior distribution of % change in admissions per 1-SD increase in each feature.\nInner bar = 50% credible interval; outer bar = 90% credible interval. \nDashed line = no effect.",
    x = "% Change in Admissions per 1-SD", y = NULL
  )

Convergence Checks

models <- list(Base = m_base, Scorecard = m_scorecard)

map_df(names(models), function(nm) {
  draws     <- as_draws_df(models[[nm]])
  rhat_vals <- summarise_draws(draws, rhat)$rhat
  ess_vals  <- summarise_draws(draws, ess_bulk)$ess_bulk
  tibble(
    Model        = nm,
    max_rhat     = round(max(rhat_vals, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    min_ess_bulk = round(min(ess_vals,  na.rm = TRUE))
  )
})

Posterior Predictive Evaluation on Test Set (2024)

Compare m_base and m_scorecard predictions against held-out actuals. Coverage: what % of actuals fall within the 90% posterior predictive interval. RMSE: root mean squared error on point estimates (posterior median).

pred_base      <- posterior_predict(m_base,      newdata = test)
pred_scorecard <- posterior_predict(m_scorecard, newdata = test)

eval_model <- function(pred_mat, label) {
  test %>%
    mutate(
      pred_median = apply(pred_mat, 2, median),
      pred_lower  = apply(pred_mat, 2, quantile, 0.05),
      pred_upper  = apply(pred_mat, 2, quantile, 0.95),
      residual    = toc_acute - pred_median,
      covered     = toc_acute >= pred_lower & toc_acute <= pred_upper,
      model       = label
    )
}

eval_base      <- eval_model(pred_base,      "Base")
eval_scorecard <- eval_model(pred_scorecard, "Scorecard")

# Summary metrics
bind_rows(eval_base, eval_scorecard) %>%
  group_by(model) %>%
  summarise(
    `90% PI Coverage` = scales::percent(mean(covered), accuracy = 0.1),
    RMSE              = round(sqrt(mean(residual^2))),
    MAE               = round(mean(abs(residual))),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
# Predicted vs actual by facility
bind_rows(eval_base, eval_scorecard) %>%
  mutate(data_year = factor(data_year)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = pred_median, y = toc_acute, color = model)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Base" = "#2c7bb6", "Scorecard" = "#d7191c")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(lineheight = 1.3)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Predicted vs Actual Acute Admissions — Test Set (2024)",
    subtitle = "Points on the dashed line = perfect prediction.\nScatter above/below = over/under-prediction per facility-year.",
    x = "Predicted (posterior median)", y = "Actual", color = "Model"
  )

# Per-facility 90% PI plot — shows coverage and direction of error
eval_scorecard %>%
  mutate(
    facility_label = fct_reorder(facility_label, pred_median),
    covered        = toc_acute >= pred_lower & toc_acute <= pred_upper
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(y = facility_label)) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = pred_lower, xend = pred_upper, yend = facility_label),
               color = "grey70", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(aes(x = pred_median), color = "#d7191c", size = 2.5) +
  geom_point(aes(x = toc_acute, shape = covered), size = 2.5, color = "black") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("TRUE" = 16, "FALSE" = 4),
                     labels = c("TRUE" = "Inside PI", "FALSE" = "Outside PI")) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(lineheight = 1.3),
        legend.position = "bottom") +
  labs(
    title    = "Posterior Credible Intervals vs Actuals — Test Set (2024)",
    subtitle = "Red dot = predicted median; black dot = actual 2024 admissions.\nFilled circle = actual falls inside 90% CI; X = outside. \nScorecard model, all 21 facilities.",
    x = "Annual Admissions", y = NULL, shape = "Actual"
  )

# Residuals by facility — signed error (actual - predicted)
eval_scorecard %>%
  mutate(
    facility_label = fct_reorder(facility_label, residual),
    direction      = if_else(residual >= 0, "Under-predicted", "Over-predicted")
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = residual, y = facility_label, fill = direction)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.8, color = "grey30") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Under-predicted" = "#d7191c", "Over-predicted" = "#2c7bb6")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(lineheight = 1.3),
        legend.position = "bottom") +
  labs(
    title    = "Forecast Residuals by Facility — Test Set (2024)",
    subtitle = "Residual = actual - predicted median. Red = model undershot actual volume; blue = model overshot.\nLarge positive residuals flag facilities the model systematically underestimates.",
    x = "Residual (Actual - Predicted)", y = NULL, fill = NULL
  )

Q2: Feature Contribution to Predictive Accuracy

LOO comparison of m_base vs m_scorecard on the training data. Positive elpd_diff for Scorecard = scorecard features improve prediction.

loo_base      <- loo(m_base)
loo_scorecard <- loo(m_scorecard)

loo_compare(loo_base, loo_scorecard)
            elpd_diff se_diff
m_scorecard  0.0       0.0   
m_base      -0.1       0.3

Q1: Facility Vulnerability Rankings (2024)

Facilities ranked by predicted 2024 acute admissions volume (posterior median). Higher predicted volume + higher baseline occupancy = greater surge exposure. The 90% posterior predictive interval reflects forecast uncertainty per facility.

vulnerability <- eval_scorecard %>%
  group_by(facility_label) %>%
  summarise(
    pred_median   = mean(pred_median),
    pred_lower    = mean(pred_lower),
    pred_upper    = mean(pred_upper),
    actual_mean   = mean(toc_acute),
    residual_mean = mean(residual),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    scorecard %>%
      mutate(facility_label = facility_labels[as.character(oshpd_id)]) %>%
      select(facility_label, occupancy_raw, share_60plus_raw),
    by = "facility_label"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    occupancy_tier = case_when(
      occupancy_raw >= quantile(occupancy_raw, 0.67, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "High",
      occupancy_raw >= quantile(occupancy_raw, 0.33, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "Medium",
      TRUE ~ "Low"
    ),
    occupancy_tier = factor(occupancy_tier, levels = c("High", "Medium", "Low")),
    facility_label = fct_reorder(facility_label, pred_median)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(pred_median))

ggplot(vulnerability, aes(x = pred_median, y = facility_label)) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = pred_lower, xend = pred_upper, yend = facility_label),
               color = "grey70", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(aes(color = occupancy_tier), size = 3) +
  geom_point(aes(x = actual_mean), shape = 4, size = 2, color = "black") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("High" = "#d7191c", "Medium" = "#fdae61",
                                "Low"  = "#2c7bb6")) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(lineheight = 1.3)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Facility Vulnerability Rankings — Predicted 2024 Volume",
    subtitle = "Line = 90% posterior predictive interval; dot = predicted median; x = actual (2024).\nModel underpredicts the highest-volume facilities (actuals fall right of interval).\nColor = baseline occupancy tier (2018-2019). High occupancy + high volume = greatest surge exposure.",
    x = "Predicted Annual Acute Admissions", y = NULL,
    color = "Baseline\nOccupancy"
  )


# Summary table
vulnerability %>%
  arrange(desc(pred_median)) %>%
  mutate(
    across(c(pred_median, pred_lower, pred_upper, actual_mean), ~ round(., 0)),
    residual_mean    = round(residual_mean, 0),
    occupancy_raw    = scales::percent(occupancy_raw, accuracy = 0.1),
    share_60plus_raw = scales::percent(share_60plus_raw, accuracy = 0.1)
  ) %>%
  select(facility_label, pred_median, pred_lower, pred_upper,
         actual_mean, residual_mean, occupancy_raw, share_60plus_raw, occupancy_tier) %>%
  rename(
    Facility         = facility_label,
    `Pred Median`    = pred_median,
    `90% Lower`      = pred_lower,
    `90% Upper`      = pred_upper,
    `Actual Mean`    = actual_mean,
    `Residual`       = residual_mean,
    `Occupancy`      = occupancy_raw,
    `60+ Share`      = share_60plus_raw,
    `Occupancy Tier` = occupancy_tier
  )

Practical Monitoring Framework

The scorecard model supports a real-time or batch vulnerability monitoring workflow. A facility is flagged as critical when its predicted annual admissions exceed the volume corresponding to 95% bed occupancy, derived from its own licensed bed size and average length of stay (2018-2023).

Capacity threshold per facility: ALOS_j = discharge_days_j / toc_acute_j (avg days per admission) threshold_j = (bed_midpoint_j x 365 x 0.95) / ALOS_j (admissions at 95% occupancy)

# Step 1: compute facility-specific ALOS from full training period (2018-2023)
alos_train <- train %>%
  group_by(oshpd_id, facility_label) %>%
  summarise(
    alos         = mean(dsch_days / toc_acute, na.rm = TRUE),
    bed_midpoint = mean(bed_midpoint,          na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # Annual admissions at which facility hits 95% licensed bed occupancy
    capacity_threshold = (bed_midpoint * 365 * 0.95) / alos
  )

cat("Facility-specific capacity thresholds (95% occupancy):\n")
Facility-specific capacity thresholds (95% occupancy):
alos_train %>%
  mutate(
    alos               = round(alos, 1),
    bed_midpoint       = round(bed_midpoint),
    capacity_threshold = round(capacity_threshold)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(capacity_threshold)) %>%
  select(facility_label, bed_midpoint, alos, capacity_threshold) %>%
  rename(
    Facility    = facility_label,
    `Beds`      = bed_midpoint,
    `ALOS`      = alos,
    `Threshold` = capacity_threshold
  ) %>%
  print(n = Inf)
# A tibble: 21 × 4
   Facility               Beds  ALOS Threshold
   <chr>                 <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl>
 1 St. Joseph              400   3.9     35732
 2 Hoag Newport Beach      400   3.9     35633
 3 Mission Mission Viejo   400   4.2     32861
 4 St. Jude                400   4.7     29706
 5 Kaiser Anaheim          250   3.6     24223
 6 Saddleback              250   3.6     23994
 7 Orange Coast Memorial   250   3.9     22367
 8 UCI Orange              400   6.4     21614
 9 Anaheim Regional        250   4.3     20045
10 UCI Fountain Valley     250   4.7     18490
11 OC Global               250   5.3     16242
12 Garden Grove            175   4.3     13964
13 West Anaheim            250   6.6     13165
14 UCI Placentia Linda     125   3.6     12022
15 UCI Los Alamitos        175   5.4     11311
16 Foothill Regional       175   6.2      9772
17 Mission Laguna Beach    175   7.6      8026
18 La Palma                125   5.4      7987
19 South Coast Global      175   8.9      6813
20 Huntington Beach        125   8.8      4927
21 Anaheim Global          175  16.1      3769
# Step 2: construct forecast data (next year beyond test)
new_data <- test %>%
  filter(data_year == max(data_year)) %>%
  mutate(year_idx = max(year_idx) + 1)

# Step 3: posterior predictive distribution for each facility
pred_new <- posterior_predict(m_scorecard, newdata = new_data)

# Step 4: P(admissions > facility-specific 95% capacity threshold)
surge_risk <- new_data %>%
  left_join(alos_train %>% select(oshpd_id, capacity_threshold),
            by = "oshpd_id") %>%
  mutate(
    pred_median = apply(pred_new, 2, median),
    pred_lower  = apply(pred_new, 2, quantile, 0.05),
    pred_upper  = apply(pred_new, 2, quantile, 0.95),
    p_critical  = map_dbl(seq_len(n()),
                          ~ mean(pred_new[, .x] > capacity_threshold[.x]))
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(p_critical))

surge_risk %>%
  mutate(
    pred_median        = scales::comma(round(pred_median)),
    capacity_threshold = scales::comma(round(capacity_threshold)),
    p_critical         = scales::percent(p_critical, accuracy = 0.1)
  ) %>%
  rename(
    Facility            = facility_label,
    `Predicted Volume`  = pred_median,
    `95% Cap Threshold` = capacity_threshold,
    `P(Critical)`       = p_critical
  ) %>%
  select(Facility, `Predicted Volume`, `95% Cap Threshold`, `P(Critical)`)
surge_risk %>%
  filter(p_critical > 0) %>%
  mutate(
    facility_label = fct_reorder(facility_label, p_critical),
    alert_tier     = case_when(
      p_critical >= 0.50 ~ "Critical (>= 50%)",
      p_critical >= 0.20 ~ "Elevated (20-49%)",
      TRUE               ~ "Watch (< 20%)"
    ),
    alert_tier = factor(alert_tier,
                        levels = c("Critical (>= 50%)", "Elevated (20-49%)", "Watch (< 20%)"))
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = p_critical, y = facility_label, fill = alert_tier)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.50, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey30", linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::percent(p_critical, accuracy = 0.1)),
            hjust = -0.15, size = 3.2, color = "grey20") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent, expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Critical (>= 50%)"  = "#d7191c",
    "Elevated (20-49%)"  = "#fdae61",
    "Watch (< 20%)"      = "#abd9e9"
  )) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(lineheight = 1.3),
        legend.position = "bottom") +
  labs(
    title    = "Surge Risk Monitor — P(Exceeding 95% Capacity) by Facility",
    subtitle = "Probability that forecasted admissions exceed the facility-specific 95% \n bed occupancy threshold. Dashed line = 50% critical threshold. \n Only facilities with P(Critical) > 0 shown.",
    x = "P(Critical)", y = NULL, fill = "Alert Tier"
  )
